backlog.title: Phase 1: Critical Bug Fixes - Block Release
backlog.description: Address critical PRD violations that prevent the system from meeting core specifications. These issues must be resolved before the implementation can be considered production-ready.

milestones.title: Milestone 1.1: Traditional Restart Logic Implementation
milestones.description: Implement traditional restart logic separate from the reflection-based retry system. This addresses PRD Section 11 requirements for parent-driven restart decisions with state restoration.

tasks.title: Add Restart Configuration to Step Decorator
tasks.description: Extend the @Step decorator to support restartable steps with configurable retry behavior.

subtasks.title: Extend StepOptions interface with restart configuration
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/restart_logic_analysis.md - @Step decorator currently has no restartable option. StepOptions interface is in src/decorators/step.ts and src/types/decorators.ts.
2. INPUT: PRD Section 11 specification for restart behavior.
3. LOGIC: Add restartable?: boolean (default: false), maxRetries?: number (default: 3), retryDelayMs?: number (default: 1000), retryOn?: ErrorCriterion[] to StepOptions interface. Define ErrorCriterion as discriminated union: { code: string } | { recoverable: boolean } | ((error: WorkflowError) => boolean).
4. OUTPUT: Updated StepOptions interface exported from src/types/decorators.ts. Type definition must be compatible with existing step decorator usage (backward compatible).

subtasks.title: Modify step decorator to implement retry loop
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/restart_logic_analysis.md - Step decorator in src/decorators/step.ts currently wraps methods in try-catch but does NOT implement retry logic. It creates WorkflowError and re-throws immediately.
2. INPUT: Extended StepOptions interface from P1.M1.T1.S1. Original method function from decorator target.
3. LOGIC: Wrap existing try-catch in while loop that checks retryCount <= maxRetries. On catch, check if opts.restartable is true. If not restartable or max retries exceeded, throw WorkflowError immediately. If restartable, emit stepRetry event (needs new event type in src/types/events.ts), wait for retryDelayMs using delay utility, increment retryCount, and continue loop. Preserve original error context.
4. OUTPUT: Modified step decorator function in src/decorators/step.ts that implements retry loop. Must emit stepRetry event with structure: { stepName: string, retryCount: number, analysis: RestartAnalysis, error: WorkflowError }.

subtasks.title: Create stepRetry event type in events schema
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/system_context.md - WorkflowEvent types are defined in src/types/events.ts. Current event types include workflow lifecycle, agent, tool, reflection, and cache events. No stepRetry event exists.
2. INPUT: Event structure requirements from P1.M1.T1.S2.
3. LOGIC: Add new discriminated union member to WorkflowEvent type: { type: 'stepRetry', stepName: string, retryCount: number, analysis: RestartAnalysis, error: WorkflowError, timestamp: number }. Ensure it follows existing event pattern with type field for discrimination.
4. OUTPUT: Updated WorkflowEvent type in src/types/events.ts with stepRetry event. Must be compatible with observer pattern (observers receive this via onEvent callback).

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for restartable step decorator
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From test suite analysis - Test files are in src/__tests__/unit/decorators.test.ts. Existing tests use AAA pattern (Arrange-Act-Assert) with helper functions. No restart logic tests currently exist.
2. INPUT: Modified step decorator from P1.M1.T1.S2.
3. LOGIC: Create test suite in src/__tests__/unit/decorators/step-restart.test.ts (NEW FILE). Test cases: should not retry step when restartable=false, should retry step when restartable=true and error occurs, should respect maxRetries limit, should emit stepRetry event on each retry, should honor retryDelayMs between retries. Mock WorkflowError creation. Use Jest timers for delay testing. Verify stepRetry events are captured by observer.
4. OUTPUT: New test file src/__tests__/unit/decorators/step-restart.test.ts with passing tests. Must follow existing test patterns (describe/it hierarchy, AAA pattern, helper functions).

tasks.title: Implement Error Analysis Utility for Restart Decisions
tasks.description: Create utility to analyze WorkflowErrors and determine if restart is appropriate based on error characteristics and step configuration.

subtasks.title: Define RestartAnalysis and ErrorCriterion interfaces
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/restart_logic_analysis.md - No restart analysis interfaces currently exist. WorkflowError interface is in src/types/error.ts with fields: message, original, workflowId, stack, state, logs.
2. INPUT: WorkflowError interface from src/types/error.ts. PRD Section 11 requirements for error analysis.
3. LOGIC: Define RestartAnalysis interface with fields: shouldRestart: boolean, reason: string, suggestedAction: 'retry' | 'abort' | 'rebuild', estimatedSuccessProbability: number (0-1). Define ErrorCriterion as discriminated union used in P1.M1.T1.S1. Export from src/types/restart.ts (NEW FILE).
4. OUTPUT: New file src/types/restart.ts with RestartAnalysis and ErrorCriterion type definitions. Must be importable by decorator and workflow modules.

subtasks.title: Implement analyzeErrorForRestart utility function
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/restart_logic_analysis.md - No error analysis utility exists. Reflection-based retry in src/core/reflection.ts provides AI-powered analysis but is different from traditional restart logic.
2. INPUT: WorkflowError instance, optional StepOptions with retryOn criteria.
3. LOGIC: Create function in src/utils/restart-analysis.ts (NEW FILE). Check error.recoverable - if false, return shouldRestart=false with 'abort' action. Check error.code against transient error list (TIMEOUT, RATE_LIMIT, NETWORK_ERROR, SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE) - return shouldRestart=true with 'retry' action. If stepOptions.retryOn provided, iterate through criteria and check matches (code equality, recoverable match, or custom function evaluation). Default: shouldRestart=false.
4. OUTPUT: New file src/utils/restart-analysis.ts with exported analyzeErrorForRestart function. Must return RestartAnalysis object. Must be side-effect free (pure function).

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for error analysis utility
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From test suite analysis - Utility tests are in src/__tests__/utils/. Error-related tests exist in src/__tests__/unit/workflow-error-utils.test.ts.
2. INPUT: analyzeErrorForRestart function from P1.M1.T2.S2.
3. LOGIC: Create test file src/__tests__/unit/restart-analysis.test.ts (NEW FILE). Test cases: should return abort for non-recoverable errors, should return retry for transient error codes, should match error code criterion, should match recoverable criterion, should call custom predicate function, should default to abort when no criteria match. Mock WorkflowError objects with different codes and recoverable flags.
4. OUTPUT: New test file src/__tests__/unit/restart-analysis.test.ts with passing tests. Must cover all code paths in analyzeErrorForRestart.

tasks.title: Implement Workflow-Level Restart Methods
tasks.description: Add restartStep() and analyzeError() methods to Workflow base class for parent-driven restart decisions with state restoration.

subtasks.title: Add restartStep method to Workflow base class
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/system_context.md - Workflow base class is in src/core/workflow.ts. No restartStep method exists. Workflow has stepMetadata Map storing decorator metadata. snapshotState() method exists for capturing state.
2. INPUT: stepName: string, options: { retryCount?: number, maxRetries?: number, stateOverride?: SerializedWorkflowState }. Step metadata from this.stepMetadata.
3. LOGIC: Create async restartStep method. Get stepMeta from stepMetadata Map - if not found or !restartable, throw error. Calculate retryCount (options.retryCount + 1). Check against maxRetries - if exceeded, throw WorkflowError with max retries message. Get state snapshot (options.stateOverride || this.snapshotState()). Re-execute step by calling this[stepName]() with restored context. Return result. Emit stepRestarted event.
4. OUTPUT: Modified Workflow class in src/core/workflow.ts with new restartStep method. Must handle missing step metadata, max retries exceeded, and state restoration failures.

subtasks.title: Add analyzeError method to Workflow base class
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/restart_logic_analysis.md - No parent-level error analysis exists. Parent workflows currently receive child error events but have no framework for restart decisions.
2. INPUT: WorkflowError from child workflow.
3. LOGIC: Create analyzeError method in Workflow class. Check error.recoverable - if false, return 'abort'. Get stepName from error metadata (if available). Get stepMeta from stepMetadata. If step not found or !restartable, return 'abort'. Check stepMeta.options.retryOn criteria (if any) against error. If any criteria match, return 'retry'. Default: return 'abort'. Return type: 'retry' | 'abort' | 'rebuild'.
4. OUTPUT: Modified Workflow class in src/core/workflow.ts with new analyzeError method. Must use analyzeErrorForRestart utility from P1.M1.T2.S2 for consistency.

subtasks.title: Add stepRestarted event type
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Event types are in src/types/events.ts. Need new event for workflow-level restart completion.
2. INPUT: Event structure from P1.M1.T3.S1.
3. LOGIC: Add new discriminated union member to WorkflowEvent type: { type: 'stepRestarted', stepName: string, retryCount: number, restoredState: SerializedWorkflowState, timestamp: number }.
4. OUTPUT: Updated WorkflowEvent type in src/types/events.ts with stepRestarted event.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for workflow restart methods
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From test suite analysis - Workflow tests are in src/__tests__/unit/workflow.test.ts. Tests use helper functions to create test workflows. No restart tests exist.
2. INPUT: Modified Workflow class with restartStep and analyzeError methods.
3. LOGIC: Create test file src/__tests__/unit/workflow-restart.test.ts (NEW FILE). Test cases: restartStep should throw error for non-restartable step, restartStep should re-execute step and return result, restartStep should restore state from snapshot, restartStep should throw error when max retries exceeded, analyzeError should return abort for non-recoverable errors, analyzeError should return retry for restartable steps with matching criteria. Create test workflow class with @Step decorated methods.
4. OUTPUT: New test file src/__tests__/unit/workflow-restart.test.ts with passing tests. Must verify state restoration, retry counting, and event emission.

tasks.title: Implement Parent-Driven Restart Decision Pattern
tasks.description: Enable parent workflows to analyze child errors and orchestrate restart decisions using the new restart infrastructure.

subtasks.title: Create parent-child restart integration test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From test suite analysis - Integration tests are in src/__tests__/integration/. Workflow tree structure tests exist in tree-mirroring.test.ts. Parent-child relationship tests exist.
2. INPUT: Workflow restart methods from P1.M1.T3, step decorator with restartable option from P1.M1.T1.
3. LOGIC: Create test file src/__tests__/integration/parent-restart-decisions.test.ts (NEW FILE). Test scenario: ChildWorkflow with @Step(restartable: true) that fails on first attempt, succeeds on second. ParentWorkflow with @Task that spawns child. Parent catches child error, calls this.analyzeError(error), if returns 'retry', calls child.restartStep(). Verify child step is re-executed and succeeds. Verify events are emitted to parent observer. Verify state is preserved across restart.
4. OUTPUT: New integration test file demonstrating parent-driven restart pattern. Must verify error propagation, analysis, decision, and re-execution flow.

subtasks.title: Document restart decision pattern with examples
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/restart_logic_analysis.md - PRD Section 11 specifies parent-driven restart but no implementation examples exist. Documentation is needed.
2. INPUT: Working restart implementation from P1.M1.T1-T3.
3. LOGIC: Create documentation file docs/restart-pattern.md (NEW FILE). Document: How to mark steps as restartable, how parent workflows analyze child errors, how to call restartStep, error criteria configuration, state restoration behavior, differences between restart and reflection-based retry. Provide code examples for common patterns (transient error retry, recoverable error retry, custom retry logic).
4. OUTPUT: New documentation file with comprehensive restart pattern guide. Must include practical examples and best practices.

milestones.title: Milestone 1.2: Workflow-Level Agent Response Validation
milestones.description: Add workflow-level validation for AgentResponse to ensure data integrity when agents are used within workflows. Addresses PRD Section 6.6 requirements.

tasks.title: Add validateAgentResponse Method to Workflow Class
tasks.description: Implement workflow-level validation that wraps Agent.validateResponse with workflow-specific error handling and event emission.

subtasks.title: Implement validateAgentResponse method in Workflow class
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/system_context.md - Agent.validateResponse() exists in src/core/agent.ts. Workflow class in src/core/workflow.ts has no validation method. AgentResponse schema validation is agent-level only.
2. INPUT: response: AgentResponse<T>, agentId: string.
3. LOGIC: Create validateAgentResponse method in Workflow class. Call Agent.validateResponse() utility (extract to shared location if needed). If validation passes, return true. If validation fails, emit invalidResponse event with structure: { response, agentId, errors: ZodError }. Create INVALID_RESPONSE_FORMAT WorkflowError with context. Return false. Method should be callable from workflow steps.
4. OUTPUT: Modified Workflow class in src/core/workflow.ts with validateAgentResponse method. Must integrate with existing event system and error handling.

subtasks.title: Add invalidResponse event type
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Event types are in src/types/events.ts. No invalidResponse event exists.
2. INPUT: Event structure requirements from P1.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Add new discriminated union member to WorkflowEvent type: { type: 'invalidResponse', response: AgentResponse, agentId: string, errors: ZodError, timestamp: number }.
4. OUTPUT: Updated WorkflowEvent type in src/types/events.ts with invalidResponse event.

subtasks.title: Extract Agent.validateResponse to shared utility
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/system_context.md - Agent.validateResponse() is in src/core/agent.ts. It's a method on Agent class but should be accessible from Workflow class without Agent instance.
2. INPUT: Existing validateResponse method from Agent class.
3. LOGIC: Extract validation logic to src/utils/agent-validation.ts (NEW FILE). Create function validateAgentResponse(response: AgentResponse): { valid: boolean, errors?: ZodError }. Keep Agent.validateResponse() as wrapper that calls this utility. Update Workflow class to use utility directly.
4. OUTPUT: New utility file src/utils/agent-validation.ts with extracted validation logic. Modified Agent class to use utility. Must maintain backward compatibility.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for workflow-level validation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Agent response validation tests exist in src/__tests__/unit/agent-response.test.ts. No workflow-level validation tests exist.
2. INPUT: validateAgentResponse method from P1.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create test file src/__tests__/unit/workflow-validation.test.ts (NEW FILE). Test cases: should return true for valid response, should return false and emit invalidResponse event for malformed response, should create INVALID_RESPONSE_FORMAT error with proper context. Create test workflow with agent. Mock agent.prompt() to return invalid responses (missing fields, wrong types, status/data mismatch). Verify events are captured.
4. OUTPUT: New test file src/__tests__/unit/workflow-validation.test.ts with passing tests. Must cover all validation failure scenarios.

tasks.title: Integrate Validation into Workflow Step Execution
tasks.description: Automatically validate agent responses when agents are used within workflow steps, with proper error handling and recovery.

subtasks.title: Add automatic validation hook to workflow context
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/system_context.md - Workflow execution context is in src/core/workflow-context.ts. Agent operations go through context. No automatic validation hook exists.
2. INPUT: validateAgentResponse utility from P1.M2.T1.S3.
3. LOGIC: Modify workflow context to automatically validate responses after agent.prompt() or agent.execute() calls. In context execution wrapper, check if result is AgentResponse. If yes, call validateAgentResponse(). If validation fails, throw INVALID_RESPONSE_FORMAT WorkflowError. Make validation opt-in via WorkflowConfig option (autoValidateResponses: boolean, default: true).
4. OUTPUT: Modified workflow context in src/core/workflow-context.ts with automatic validation hook. Must not break existing agent usage patterns.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for automatic validation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Integration tests are in src/__tests__/integration/. Agent-workflow integration tests exist in agent-workflow.test.ts.
2. INPUT: Modified workflow context with automatic validation from P1.M2.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create test file src/__tests__/integration/agent-validation.test.ts (NEW FILE). Test cases: should automatically validate valid agent responses in workflow, should throw INVALID_RESPONSE_FORMAT for invalid responses, should emit invalidResponse event, should allow disabling auto-validation via config, should handle validation errors gracefully. Create workflow with @Step that calls agent.prompt(). Mock agent to return valid and invalid responses.
4. OUTPUT: New integration test file src/__tests__/integration/agent-validation.test.ts with passing tests. Must verify automatic validation behavior and event emission.

subtasks.title: Document workflow-level validation behavior
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Documentation gap exists - PRD Section 6.6 requires workflow-level validation but no docs explain usage.
2. INPUT: Working validation implementation from P1.M2.T1-T2.
3. LOGIC: Update docs/workflows.md (or create if doesn't exist) with section on agent response validation. Document: automatic validation behavior, how to disable, manual validation with validateAgentResponse(), invalidResponse event structure, INVALID_RESPONSE_FORMAT error handling. Provide code examples for manual validation in error handlers.
4. OUTPUT: Updated documentation with validation section. Must be clear and include practical examples.

milestones.title: Milestone 1.3: OpenCode Provider Decision and Resolution
milestones.description: Decide on the future of OpenCode provider based on feature parity requirements. Choose between: A) Document limitations clearly, B) Implement full MCP/LSP support, or C) Remove provider.

tasks.title: Decision: OpenCode Provider Future
tasks.description: Make architectural decision on OpenCode provider based on PRD requirements, implementation effort, and business impact. This is a planning task, not an implementation task.

subtasks.title: Create decision analysis document
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/external_deps.md and provider system analysis - OpenCode provider has critical gaps (no MCP/LSP) vs PRD requirements. Three options exist: A) Document limitations, B) Implement full support (4-6 weeks), C) Remove provider.
2. INPUT: PRD Section 7.4 capabilities specification, provider implementation analysis from architecture research.
3. LOGIC: Create decision document plan/003_dd63ad365ffb/bugfix/001_45bfbada88e7/opencode-decision.md (NEW FILE). Analyze: Effort for each option (A: 1-2 days docs, B: 4-6 weeks dev, C: 1 week deprecation), Business impact (multi-provider promise, user expectations), Technical feasibility (SDK limitations, breaking changes), Test coverage implications. Provide recommendation with rationale.
4. OUTPUT: Decision analysis document with clear recommendation. Must be actionable for product/engineering leadership.

subtasks.title: Execute OpenCode provider resolution
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This subtask implementation depends on decision from P1.M3.T1.S1. Cannot specify implementation without knowing decision.
2. INPUT: Decision document from P1.M3.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: [TO BE DETERMINED BASED ON DECISION]. If Option A (Document): Update docs/providers.md with prominent warnings, update PRD capabilities table, add deprecation notice. If Option B (Implement): Create new tasks for MCP/LSP implementation (out of scope for current PRD). If Option C (Remove): Deprecate provider, add migration guide, remove from registry.
4. OUTPUT: [TO BE DETERMINED]. Resolution of OpenCode provider feature parity violation.

backlog.title: Phase 2: Major Issues - Next Release
backlog.description: Address major issues that impact system quality and type safety but do not completely block release. These should be resolved in the next release cycle.

milestones.title: Milestone 2.1: Provider Interface Type Consistency
milestones.description: Fix type system inconsistency where Provider interface specifies ProviderResult<T> but implementations return AgentResponse<T>. This breaks polymorphic usage.

tasks.title: Align Provider Interface Return Type with Implementations
tasks.description: Update Provider interface to return AgentResponse<T> consistently with actual implementations, deprecating unused ProviderResult<T> type.

subtasks.title: Update Provider interface execute() return type
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From provider analysis - Provider interface in src/types/providers.ts line 559 specifies Promise<ProviderResult<T>> but implementations return Promise<AgentResponse<T>>. ProviderResult is defined but unused.
2. INPUT: Current Provider interface definition.
3. LOGIC: Modify Provider.execute() method signature to return Promise<AgentResponse<T>> | AsyncGenerator<StreamEvent, AgentResponse<T>> to match actual implementations. Add @deprecated JSDoc comment to ProviderResult type definition pointing to AgentResponse.
4. OUTPUT: Updated Provider interface in src/types/providers.ts with correct return type. Deprecated ProviderResult type with migration guidance.

subtasks.title: Update PRD documentation for Provider interface
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD Section 7.3 specifies ProviderResult return type. Documentation must match implementation.
2. INPUT: Updated Provider interface from P2.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Update PRD.md Section 7.3 to reflect AgentResponse<T> return type. Add migration note for code using ProviderResult. Update examples to use AgentResponse.
4. OUTPUT: Updated PRD documentation matching implementation reality.

subtasks.title: Add type compatibility tests for Provider interface
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Provider interface tests exist in src/__tests__/unit/provider-interface.test.ts. No tests verify return type consistency.
2. INPUT: Updated Provider interface from P2.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Extend existing provider interface tests. Add test: polymorphic usage should work correctly - create Provider variable, assign AnthropicProvider instance, call execute() and verify return type is AgentResponse. Ensure TypeScript compilation passes with strict type checking.
4. OUTPUT: Extended test coverage verifying type consistency. Must compile without type errors.

milestones.title: Milestone 2.2: Session Persistence Implementation
milestones.description: Implement session persistence to maintain conversation state across provider lifecycle (initialize → terminate → initialize). Currently sessions are in-memory only and lost on terminate.

tasks.title: Design Session Storage Abstraction Layer
tasks.description: Create abstraction layer for session storage with pluggable backends (memory, file, Redis) to support different deployment scenarios.

subtasks.title: Define SessionStore interface and implementations
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/external_deps.md - No session persistence exists. AnthropicProvider uses Map<string, SessionState> for in-memory storage. SessionState interface exists in anthropic-provider.ts.
2. INPUT: Existing SessionState interface from anthropic-provider.ts.
3. LOGIC: Create src/providers/session-store.ts (NEW FILE). Define SessionStore interface with methods: save(sessionId: string, state: SessionState): Promise<void>, load(sessionId: string): Promise<SessionState | null>, delete(sessionId: string): Promise<void>, list(): Promise<string[]>. Implement MemorySessionStore (wraps Map, no persistence), FileSessionStore (uses fs.promises, JSON serialization), interface for RedisSessionStore (future).
4. OUTPUT: New session store abstraction with multiple implementations. MemorySessionStore must maintain current behavior for backward compatibility.

subtasks.title: Add session serialization utilities
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: SessionState contains SDK message objects which may not be directly serializable. Need serialization utilities.
2. INPUT: SessionState interface from anthropic-provider.ts.
3. LOGIC: Create utility functions in src/utils/session-serialization.ts (NEW FILE). serializeSession(state: SessionState): string - convert to JSON-safe format handling circular refs and special types. deserializeSession(data: string): SessionState - reconstruct SessionState from serialized data. Handle SDK message serialization (may need custom logic).
4. OUTPUT: Serialization utilities that can round-trip SessionState through JSON.

subtasks.title: Integrate session store into AnthropicProvider
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: AnthropicProvider in src/providers/anthropic-provider.ts has private sessions Map. terminate() calls sessions.clear(). initialize() creates new Map.
2. INPUT: SessionStore interface from P2.M2.T1.S1, serialization utilities from P2.M2.T1.S2.
3. LOGIC: Modify AnthropicProvider constructor to accept optional sessionStore: SessionStore (default: MemorySessionStore). Replace private sessions Map with sessionStore usage. In initialize(): if sessionStore is not MemorySessionStore, call sessionStore.list() and restore sessions. In terminate(): if sessionStore is not MemorySessionStore, save all sessions via sessionStore.save(). Update createSession(), getSession(), deleteSession() to use sessionStore API.
4. OUTPUT: Modified AnthropicProvider with pluggable session storage. Default behavior unchanged (backward compatible).

subtasks.title: Write tests for session persistence
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Session tests exist in src/__tests__/unit/providers/anthropic-provider-sessions.test.ts (728 lines). Tests verify sessions are cleared on terminate.
2. INPUT: Modified AnthropicProvider with session persistence from P2.M2.T1.S3.
3. LOGIC: Extend existing session tests. Add tests: should persist sessions across terminate → initialize cycle with FileSessionStore, should save sessions to disk, should restore sessions on initialize, should handle missing session file gracefully, should default to in-memory sessions when no store provided. Create temporary directory for file store tests. Clean up after tests.
4. OUTPUT: Extended test coverage verifying session persistence. Must test both success and failure scenarios.

tasks.title: Add Session Configuration and Lifecycle Management
tasks.description: Add configuration options for session persistence and implement session TTL, cleanup, and lifecycle management.

subtasks.title: Add session configuration to ProviderOptions
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: ProviderOptions interface exists in src/types/providers.ts. No session configuration options exist.
2. INPUT: SessionStore implementations from P2.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Extend ProviderOptions interface with: sessionStore?: SessionStore, sessionPersistence?: 'memory' | 'file' | 'redis', sessionTtl?: number (milliseconds, default: 24 hours), sessionPath?: string (for file store). Update AnthropicProvider.initialize() to read these options and configure session store accordingly.
4. OUTPUT: Extended ProviderOptions interface with session configuration. Must support easy configuration (e.g., sessionPersistence: 'file').

subtasks.title: Implement session TTL and cleanup
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: No session TTL or cleanup exists. Sessions persist indefinitely in current implementation.
2. INPUT: SessionTtl configuration from P2.M2.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: Extend SessionState with createdAt and lastAccessedAt timestamps. In FileSessionStore.save(), add/update timestamps. In FileSessionStore.load(), check if session is expired (lastAccessedAt + ttl < now). Return null if expired. Add cleanup method to SessionStore: deleteExpired(): Promise<void>. Call cleanup periodically in provider or on initialize.
4. OUTPUT: Session TTL enforcement in FileSessionStore. Must handle edge cases (clock skew, negative TTL, disabled TTL).

subtasks.title: Write documentation for session persistence
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Documentation gap exists - PRD Section 7.4 mentions sessions but persistence is not documented.
2. INPUT: Session persistence implementation from P2.M2.T1-T2.
3. LOGIC: Update docs/providers.md with session persistence section. Document: session lifecycle (create → use → persist → restore), configuration options (sessionPersistence, sessionTtl, sessionPath), differences between storage backends, session cleanup behavior, migration from in-memory to persistent sessions. Provide code examples for each backend.
4. OUTPUT: Comprehensive session persistence documentation. Must include practical examples and configuration guidance.

milestones.title: Milestone 2.3: Tree Update Event Consistency
milestones.description: Ensure treeUpdated event is emitted consistently for all state changes to maintain the PRD's '1:1 tree mirror' guarantee.

tasks.title: Audit and Fix treeUpdated Event Emission
tasks.description: Identify all state-changing methods in Workflow class and ensure treeUpdated event is emitted after structural changes.

subtasks.title: Audit state-changing methods in Workflow class
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/system_context.md - Workflow class in src/core/workflow.ts has methods that change state: setStatus(), snapshotState(), attachChild(), detachChild(). Inconsistent treeUpdated emission reported.
2. INPUT: Workflow class source code.
3. LOGIC: Read through src/core/workflow.ts and identify all methods that modify: status, stateSnapshot, children array, parent reference, logs array. Create list of methods with current treeUpdated emission status (yes/no). Document in plan/003_dd63ad365ffb/bugfix/001_45bfbada88e7/architecture/tree-update-audit.md.
4. OUTPUT: Audit document listing all state-changing methods and their treeUpdated emission status.

subtasks.title: Add treeUpdated emission to missing methods
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: treeUpdated event exists in WorkflowEvent type. Observer pattern supports onTreeChanged callback.
2. INPUT: Audit list from P2.M3.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: For each method in audit that lacks treeUpdated emission, add this.emit('treeUpdated', { root: this.toNode() }) after state change. Methods to fix likely include: setStatus(), snapshotState(), possibly attachChild()/detachChild() (check if already emitting). Ensure emission happens AFTER state is modified. Add JSDoc comments indicating treeUpdated emission.
4. OUTPUT: Modified Workflow class with consistent treeUpdated emission. All state changes must notify observers.

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for tree update consistency
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Tree mirroring tests exist in src/__tests__/integration/tree-mirroring.test.ts. Tests verify 1:1 mirror but not treeUpdated emission.
2. INPUT: Modified Workflow class from P2.M3.T1.S2.
3. LOGIC: Extend tree-mirroring.test.ts. Add tests: should emit treeUpdated when status changes, should emit treeUpdated when snapshot is created, should emit treeUpdated when child is attached, should emit treeUpdated when child is detached, observer should receive all tree updates. Create observer that records treeUpdated events and verify count matches expected.
4. OUTPUT: Extended test coverage verifying treeUpdated emission for all state changes. Must catch regression.

milestones.title: Milestone 2.4: Workflow-Level Error Merge Strategy
milestones.description: Extend error merge strategy from @Task concurrent execution to workflow-level configuration, enabling consistent error handling across the entire workflow.

tasks.title: Add Error Merge Strategy to WorkflowConfig
tasks.description: Enable error merge strategy configuration at workflow level, not just for @Task concurrent execution.

subtasks.title: Extend WorkflowConfig with errorMergeStrategy
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/system_context.md - ErrorMergeStrategy exists in src/types/error-strategy.ts. @Task decorator supports error merge for concurrent tasks. WorkflowConfig in src/types/workflow-context.ts has no error merge option.
2. INPUT: Existing ErrorMergeStrategy interface.
3. LOGIC: Extend WorkflowConfig interface in src/types/workflow-context.ts with optional errorMergeStrategy?: ErrorMergeStrategy field. Pass through to workflow initialization. Default: disabled (matches PRD - 'first error wins').
4. OUTPUT: Extended WorkflowConfig interface with error merge support.

subtasks.title: Implement error collection in workflow execution
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: mergeWorkflowErrors utility exists in src/utils/workflow-error-utils.ts. Used by @Task decorator. Not used by workflow-level execution.
2. INPUT: ErrorMergeStrategy config from P2.M4.T1.S1, mergeWorkflowErrors utility.
3. LOGIC: Modify workflow run() execution to collect errors when errorMergeStrategy.enabled is true. In sequential step execution, catch each error and add to collection instead of throwing immediately. After run() completes (or all steps fail), if errors collected and merge strategy enabled, call mergeWorkflowErrors() with collected errors. If only one error, throw it directly. If multiple errors, throw WorkflowAggregateError.
4. OUTPUT: Modified workflow execution in src/core/workflow.ts that supports error merge for sequential steps. Must not break existing behavior (default disabled).

subtasks.title: Write tests for workflow-level error merge
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Error merge strategy tests exist in src/__tests__/adversarial/error-merge-strategy.test.ts (760 lines) but only for @Task concurrent execution.
2. INPUT: Modified workflow execution from P2.M4.T1.S2.
3. LOGIC: Create test file src/__tests__/unit/workflow-error-merge.test.ts (NEW FILE). Test cases: should throw first error when merge disabled (default), should merge all sequential step errors when enabled, should use custom combine function if provided, should emit merged error event. Create test workflow with multiple @Step decorated methods that fail. Verify WorkflowAggregateError contains all errors.
4. OUTPUT: New test file with comprehensive workflow-level error merge coverage.

backlog.title: Phase 3: Minor Issues - Polish
backlog.description: Address minor issues that improve developer experience, documentation clarity, and system polish. These are low-priority but should be completed for production readiness.

milestones.title: Milestone 3.1: Documentation and DX Improvements
milestones.description: Improve documentation clarity, add helper methods for common operations, and enhance developer experience.

tasks.title: Add Provider Capability Query Helpers
tasks.description: Add convenience methods to Provider class for checking capabilities at runtime, improving developer experience.

subtasks.title: Implement supports() and requiresFeatures() methods
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: From architecture/external_deps.md - Provider.capabilities object exists but no helper methods. Users must manually check provider.capabilities.mcp.
2. INPUT: Provider interface, ProviderCapabilities type.
3. LOGIC: Add methods to Provider interface: supports(capability: keyof ProviderCapabilities): boolean - returns this.capabilities[capability], requiresFeatures(features: (keyof ProviderCapabilities)[]): boolean - returns features.every(f => this.capabilities[f]). Implement in both AnthropicProvider and OpenCodeProvider.
4. OUTPUT: Enhanced Provider interface with capability checking helpers. Must be implemented by all providers.

subtasks.title: Write tests for capability helpers
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Provider tests exist in src/__tests__/unit/providers/ directory. No capability helper tests exist.
2. INPUT: Implemented capability helpers from P3.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Extend provider tests. Add tests: supports() should return true for enabled capabilities, supports() should return false for disabled capabilities, requiresFeatures() should return true when all features present, requiresFeatures() should return false when any feature missing. Test with both AnthropicProvider and OpenCodeProvider.
4. OUTPUT: Test coverage for capability checking helpers.

tasks.title: Improve JSDoc Clarity
tasks.description: Update JSDoc comments to be clearer about default values and behavior, addressing Issue 9 from the bug report.

subtasks.title: Update Step decorator JSDoc for trackTiming default

CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Issue 9 notes that trackTiming defaults to true but JSDoc doesn't clearly state this. Step decorator in src/decorators/step.ts line 94 has unclear comment.
2. INPUT: Step decorator JSDoc comments.
3. LOGIC: Update trackTiming JSDoc from 'Track and emit step duration' to 'Track and emit step duration (default: true, tracked unless explicitly set to false)'. Make the default crystal clear.
4. OUTPUT: Improved JSDoc clarity in src/decorators/step.ts.

subtasks.title: Audit and improve other unclear JSDoc comments
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: JSDoc clarity varies across codebase. Some defaults are unclear.
2. INPUT: Codebase JSDoc comments.
3. LOGIC: Audit JSDoc in critical files: Workflow class, Agent class, Provider interface, decorators. Identify comments with unclear defaults or behavior. Update to be explicit: '(default: X)', 'required vs optional', side effects. Focus on public APIs.
4. OUTPUT: Improved JSDoc clarity across key interfaces and classes.

tasks.title: Add Workflow Name Security Validation
tasks.description: Enhance workflow name validation to prevent injection attacks and improve security (Issue 10).

subtasks.title: Implement workflow name security validation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Issue 10 - Workflow constructor validates empty/whitespace/length but not special characters. Names like '<script>alert('xss')</script>' are allowed.
2. INPUT: Workflow constructor in src/core/workflow.ts lines 99-107.
3. LOGIC: Add validation for: control characters (/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/), HTML/JS injection patterns (/<[^>]*>/, /javascript:/), path traversal (\.\.), file system operations. Throw WorkflowError with descriptive message if validation fails. Add JSDoc documenting allowed characters (alphanumeric, spaces, hyphens, underscores).
4. OUTPUT: Enhanced workflow name validation in constructor. Must catch injection patterns.

subtasks.title: Write tests for name validation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Workflow constructor tests exist but don't test security validation.
2. INPUT: Enhanced validation from P3.M1.T3.S1.
3. LOGIC: Extend workflow constructor tests. Add tests: should throw error for control characters, should throw error for HTML tags, should throw error for javascript: protocol, should throw error for path traversal patterns, should allow valid names with spaces/hyphens/underscores.
4. OUTPUT: Test coverage for workflow name security validation.

milestones.title: Milestone 3.2: AgentResponse Schema Strengthening
milestones.description: Strengthen AgentResponse JSON schema validation to enforce stricter consistency rules (Issue 7).

tasks.title: Create Discriminated Union for AgentResponse
tasks.description: Use discriminated union pattern to enforce status/error/data consistency in AgentResponse schema.

subtasks.title: Refactor AgentResponse as discriminated union
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Issue 7 - AgentResponse allows invalid combinations like status='success' with error!=null. Schema in src/types/agent.ts doesn't enforce consistency.
2. INPUT: Current AgentResponse interface.
3. LOGIC: Refactor AgentResponse to discriminated union: type SuccessResponse<T> = { status: 'success', data: T, error: null, metadata: AgentResponseMetadata }, type ErrorResponse = { status: 'error', data: null, error: AgentErrorDetails, metadata: AgentResponseMetadata }, type AgentResponse<T> = SuccessResponse<T> | ErrorResponse. This enforces type-level consistency.
4. OUTPUT: Refactored AgentResponse type in src/types/agent.ts. Must be backward compatible with existing usage.

subtasks.title: Add Zod refinement for validation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: AgentResponseSchema exists but doesn't catch invalid combinations. Zod schema in src/types/agent.ts.
2. INPUT: Refactored AgentResponse type from P3.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Update AgentResponseSchema to use discriminated union validation. Add .refine() to ensure: if status='success' then error must be null and data must be non-null, if status='error' then data must be null and error must be non-null. Add test cases for invalid combinations.
4. OUTPUT: Strengthened AgentResponseSchema that enforces consistency. Must catch previously allowed invalid responses.

subtasks.title: Write tests for schema validation
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Agent response validation tests exist in src/__tests__/unit/agent-response.test.ts but don't test invalid combinations.
2. INPUT: Strengthened schema from P3.M2.T1.S2.
3. LOGIC: Extend agent-response.test.ts. Add tests: should reject success response with error, should reject success response with null data, should reject error response with data, should reject error response with null error. Verify validation fails with descriptive error.
4. OUTPUT: Test coverage for strengthened AgentResponse validation.

milestones.title: Milestone 3.3: Event Replay System
milestones.description: Implement workflow event replay functionality to enable late-joining observers to catch up on past events (Issue 12).

tasks.title: Add Event History Storage to Workflow
tasks.description: Store event history in workflow and implement replay mechanism for observers.

subtasks.title: Implement event history storage
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Issue 12 - WorkflowEventReplayer exists in tests but not integrated into Workflow class. No API to request event replay.
2. INPUT: Workflow class in src/core/workflow.ts, WorkflowEvent type.
3. LOGIC: Add private eventHistory: WorkflowEvent[] array to Workflow class. In emit() method, push events to history before notifying observers. Add replayEvents(observer: WorkflowObserver, options?: { since?: number, limit?: number }) method that iterates through history and calls observer.onEvent() for each event. Add clearEventHistory() method.
4. OUTPUT: Modified Workflow class with event history storage and replay capability.

subtasks.title: Add event history configuration
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Event history could consume unbounded memory. Need configuration and limits.
2. INPUT: Event history implementation from P3.M3.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Add eventHistoryConfig to WorkflowConfig: { enabled: boolean (default: false), maxEvents: number (default: 1000), maxAgeMs: number (default: 3600000 - 1 hour) }. In emit(), check if history is enabled before storing. Implement trimming: if maxEvents exceeded, shift() oldest events; if maxAgeMs exceeded, filter old events. Add config option to Workflow constructor.
4. OUTPUT: Configurable event history with memory limits.

subtasks.title: Write tests for event replay
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: No event replay tests exist.
2. INPUT: Event history implementation from P3.M3.T1.S2.
3. LOGIC: Create test file src/__tests__/unit/event-replay.test.ts (NEW FILE). Test cases: should store events when history enabled, should replay events to late-joining observer, should respect maxEvents limit, should respect maxAgeMs limit, should not store events when disabled, should clear event history on request. Create test workflow, emit events, add observer after events, verify replay.
4. OUTPUT: Test coverage for event replay functionality.
